name: The Explorer
what_they_respond_to: Region storytelling, winemaker profiles, food pairing, 'what to try next' framing
segment_signals: High pdp_view count relative to purchase count
blog_view events in session history
Varietal or region-specific search queries
Low purchase frequency despite high site engagement
tone_guidance: Lead with discovery and story. Educational content converts well — they want to learn something. Use sensory language and winemaker/vineyard context. Avoid hard-sell or urgency framing.

name: The Gifter
demographics: Adults 35–55, suburban, $80k–$160k income, purchases spike Nov–Dec and around key dates
purchase_behavior: 2–4 purchases/year, AOV $60–100, prefers gift sets or well-packaged bottles, often ships to a different address
what_they_respond_to: Gift suitability cues, occasion framing (birthday, anniversary, corporate), clear value and packaging quality, fast/reliable shipping emphasis
segment_signals: Purchase history in Nov/Dec or around Valentine's/Mother's Day
Ships to multiple addresses
Selects gift wrap or gift note at checkout
Short browse-to-purchase sessions (goal-oriented)
tone_guidance: Occasion-forward framing. Lead with the recipient and the moment, not the wine. Emphasize presentation and reliability. Keep copy concise and direct — Gifters are on a mission, not browsing.

name: The Loyalist
demographics: Adults 38–60, any geography, $90k–$200k income, long customer tenure
purchase_behavior: 6+ orders/year, AOV $50–90, strong varietal/producer preferences, high email engagement
what_they_respond_to: Exclusive access, new vintages from known producers, loyalty recognition, early access to limited stock, being treated as an insider
segment_signals: RFM Champions or Loyal Customers segment
Email open rate > 35%
Purchases repeat SKUs or same producer across vintages
tone_guidance: Speak to them as a trusted insider. Acknowledge their loyalty subtly — 'As one of our most engaged customers...' Avoid generic promotional language. They respond to curation and access, not discounts.

name: The Deal Seeker
demographics: Adults 30–50, price-sensitive, any income band, high promotion sensitivity
purchase_behavior: Purchases cluster around discount events, AOV $30–60, likely to buy multiples when discounted, low base purchase frequency
what_they_respond_to: Clear discount percentages, limited-time offers, savings framing ('Save $X'), bundle deals, free shipping thresholds
segment_signals: seg-deal-seekers segment membership
Purchase history limited to promotion periods
High email click rate on discount subject lines
Abandoned carts when no promotion code applied
tone_guidance: Lead with the offer — savings first, story second. Use concrete numbers ('15% off', '$12 savings'). Urgency and scarcity language is appropriate here (unlike other personas). Avoid premium/collector positioning.

name: The Collector
demographics: Adults 40–65, high income $150k+, often in major metro areas, deep wine knowledge
purchase_behavior: 3–8 orders/year, AOV $100–250+, purchases premium/collector-tier SKUs, may buy by the case, long consideration windows
what_they_respond_to: Provenance and terroir detail, vintage specifics, critic scores, cellaring potential, winemaker philosophy, allocation scarcity
segment_signals: seg-collectors segment membership
Purchased SKUs priced > $120
Long session duration on PDP pages
Reads tasting notes and technical details
tone_guidance: Match their expertise level. Use technical detail when appropriate (vintage conditions, terroir). Never talk down or over-explain basics. Scarcity is more powerful than discount — never discount collector SKUs. Prestige framing works.
